feedback.text: A few comments on the ideas to cover: - unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns - needs a lot more clarification. Please do expand what this means in your view. - misleading marketing by influencers - clear and Obvious. Influencers need to be very clear (mandated by law) to disclose they are advertising a product or service. Also anyone advertising an item, should carry responsibility for what is said in the advertisement, if it is later proved in court it was incorrect, unless they can prove the company behind the item shared that talking point and it was part of the contract. - addictive design of digital products - there needs to be limits set in all online gambling. That includes dark patterns, like it is done in games with "loot boxes". Especially when the audience is kids. - unfair personalisation practices - personalisation for commercial reasons should be clearly banned. Not hidden behind a some "cookie policy" that is in legalese and 5 pages long. And not hidden behind "terms of service" that force you to accept personal tracking for commercial puproses or have to pay subscription fees. A few comments on the ideas to *ADD*: - the initiative of "Stop killing games" should be included in this bill. Force all new products, not just games, to have an end of life plan. What that means is when the company stops supporting a product/service to have clear way for that product to keep working, even in an offline or localy or third party hosted system. All that communicated from the moment the product goes to the market. If it is a service that requires the company to maintain equipment related to a cost, then the service provider should be communicating clearly that the service has a set timeline of operation. They should be mandated to publicly announce the end of said service 2 years in advance. And stop selling the service to new customers 1 year in advance (in a cooling off period), so people do not get attached to the service (with their personal investment in time or money for the service to stop working a month later. - When buying a digital form of anything (song, book, movie, game, etc) you should not be the holder of just a limited digital key licence, that can be cancelled at any time and the product disappear. You should have the rights to what you bought, as if it was an equal hard-copy / real physical item. It should not matter whether that item is also sold as a real life physical item or not. - When buying an item (physical product or digital software) you should have the rights of use, in the form it was bought forever. No update or change of policy or ownership in the provider company should be allowed to change the operation of the product through an update. Especially not through an automatic update, while the user is not aware of what the update carries. example: I should not be able to buy a smart bulb, that connects to my WIFI and can control it through my phone and three months later the company, that maintains the servers through which the smart bulb operate, change the control of the bulb to a subscription plan or the bulb no longer works. They should be mandated: to clearly state if a subscription is required before the sale of the item. If that is not communicated the item should be able to continue operation as it did before. If they want to provide new and extra feature in its operation they can do that under a subscription as it was not promised for the sale of the item. If they want to shut down the servers that support the item, then they should prepare an option for the item to be locally hosted (by local servers the user can set-up - prepared by the company before killing support) or by third party servers that some other company might want to provide support for. Also no update should be allowed that forces the smart bulb to completely stop working, even with a physical electrical switch if it is not connected to the internet.
